title: Quick Install adoption branch and remote E2E

sources.reason: Bare remote and Remote HEAD regression evidence.

riskAssessment.reason: Installer changes Git branch state and must remain fail-closed for dirty or ambiguous repositories.

executionDecision.reason: The branch creation and remote E2E behavior are explicitly bounded and testable.

preReviewWarnings: Verify remote HEAD discovery, fetch anchoring, branch collision handling, and rollback behavior.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: The Work Item Contract is updated for the actual task.
The new feature is implemented according to requirements.
Unit tests are added to verify the new feature.
User documentation or comments are updated.
guidelines: The installer must not commit, push, merge, or delete adopter branches.
Remote and base identity must be recorded in the generated adoption Contract.

intent.problem: Quick Install currently writes adoption files on whichever branch the adopter happens to have checked out, so the generated Work Item can conflict with the required one-Work-Item/one-branch lifecycle and can record a stale base.
constraints: Do not commit, push, merge, or delete adopter branches from the installer.
Preserve local-only test fixtures while making real remotes deterministic.
Do not claim branch creation replaces PR review or lifecycle closure.
intent.rationale: Discovering the remote HEAD, fetching its default branch, and creating a dedicated adoption branch before writes makes the documented Quick Install path produce an auditable branch/base contract.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: User authorized full automated execution of the review remediation work items.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
